2604.12762.title: ARGOS: Who, Where, and When in Agentic Multi-Camera Person Search
2604.12762.paper_summary: We introduce ARGOS, the first benchmark and framework that reformulates multi-camera person search as an interactive reasoning problem requiring an agent to plan, question, and eliminate candidates under information asymmetry. An ARGOS agent receives a vague witness statement and must decide what to ask, when to invoke spatial or temporal tools, and how to interpret ambiguous responses, all within a limited turn budget. Reasoning is grounded in a Spatio-Temporal Topology Graph (STTG) encoding camera connectivity and empirically validated transition times. The benchmark comprises 2,691 tasks across 14 real-world scenarios in three progressive tracks: semantic perception (Who), spatial reasoning (Where), and temporal reasoning (When). Experiments with four LLM backbones show the benchmark is far from solved (best TWS: 0.383 on Track 2, 0.590 on Track 3), and ablations confirm that removing domain-specific tools drops accuracy by up to 49.6 percentage points.
2604.12762.paper_summary_zh: 我們介紹ARGOS，第一個基準和框架，將多攝影機人員搜索重新定義為一個互動推理問題，要求代理人計劃、提問並在信息不對稱的情況下排除候選者。ARGOS代理人接收模糊的證人陳述，必須決定要問什麼、何時使用空間或時間工具，以及如何解釋模棱兩可的回應，所有這些都在有限的回合預算內進行。推理基於一個時空拓撲圖（STTG），編碼攝影機連接性和經驗驗證的轉換時間。該基準包含2,691個任務，涵蓋14個現實世界場景，分為三個漸進的軌道：語義感知（誰）、空間推理（哪裡）和時間推理（何時）。對四個LLM骨幹的實驗顯示，該基準遠未解決（最佳TWS：第2軌道0.383，第3軌道0.590），而消融實驗確認移除特定領域工具會使準確率下降多達49.6個百分點。
2604.12762.author: Myungchul Kim et.al.
2604.12762.authors: Myungchul Kim, Kwanyong Park, Junmo Kim, In So Kweon
